statistical_gate.method: bootstrap percentile 95% CI, 10000 resamples, seed=4242; pass = CI_low >= threshold

n_policy.note: N=10 < 30: iteration run only. NOT ship-gate eligible — CIs too wide to certify a pass.

rows.judge_notes: Citation relevance is low: citations 1-7 are generic Kamino staking-yield API entries for unrelated token mints (not the JLP-USDC multiply vault specifically), and citations 8-12 are canon boilerplate (Berkshire 1978 letter with garbled binary, Mauboussin on confidence, Damodaran on real options, BIS stablecoin paper, Marks memo) with no direct bearing on JLP-USDC multiply vault parameters or leverage mechanics. No citation directly addresses the JLP-USDC multiply vault. Hallucination score is clean: no specific ungrounded USD price, TVL, or APY figure for the JLP-USDC multiply vault appears in the verdict text itself. Dissent grounding is 0: all 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, so none of the expected dissent topics (smart_contract_risk, leverage_unwind_risk, vault_parameter_change_risk) were surfaced in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or critic turns — blocker_questions list is empty and dissent_count is 0. Confidence calibration is poor: a 0.5 confidence DEFER verdict was issued with zero functioning agent turns and no substantive analysis, making the confidence figure essentially arbitrary rather than calibrated to citation strength or dissent signals.

rows.judge_notes: Citations 1-7 are Kamino protocol-native market data (on-topic for the protocol/vertical). Citations 8-12 are canon boilerplate (Marks, Damodaran, Mauboussin, Fed paper, Berkshire letter) with only tangential relevance to the specific leverage question — citation 12 is garbled binary. No specific ungrounded price/APY/liquidation figures appear in the verdict text, so no hallucination pattern fires. However, all 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, meaning no dissent was actually surfaced — none of the expected dissent topics (liquidation_cascade_risk, drawdown_path_risk, audit_status_uncertainty, leverage_unwind_risk) appear in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or any critic turn. dissent_count=0 and blocker_questions=[] confirm this. Confidence of 0.5 with a DEFER verdict is directionally reasonable given the structural risk of 3x leverage, but the panel produced no substantive reasoning to justify even that calibration — all voices failed, making the confidence somewhat arbitrary rather than well-grounded.

rows.judge_notes: Citation relevance: Citations 1 and 3 are directly on-topic (Kamino market API and JitoSOL staking yields). Citations 4-7 reference other LST staking yields not directly relevant to JitoSOL-SOL vault comparison. Citations 2 references MSOL-SOL market (tangential). Citations 8-12 are generic canon boilerplate (Damodaran, BIS, Mauboussin, Marks, Berkshire) with zero relevance to Kamino/JitoSOL/SOL vault mechanics — dragging relevance down significantly. Hallucination: No specific APY differential, peg figure, or guaranteed outperformance claim appears in the verdict text; the staking yield figures in citations 3-7 are sourced from protocol_native API with daily freshness, so they are grounded. No must_not_hallucinate pattern triggered. Dissent grounding: All 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError — no dissent was produced at all. None of the expected dissent topics (marginal_yield_uplift_risk, peg_deviation_risk, transaction_cost_overhead) appear in blocker_questions (empty) or surviving dissent (dissent_count=0). Score 0.0. Confidence calibration: Panel returned 0.5 confidence with a DEFER verdict, which is directionally reasonable given the thin-yield context and the why_context note. However, all agent voices failed, so the confidence is essentially ungrounded by any deliberation — mild miscalibration, not severe, since 0.5 is a neutral/cautious level consistent with DEFER.

rows.judge_notes: Citation relevance: Citations 1-7 are Kamino staking-yield API pulls (protocol-native, daily) and are broadly on-topic for Kamino yield questions, but none are specifically tied to the USDC-PYUSD vault; the token mints are unidentified and may correspond to LST vaults rather than the stablecoin pair in question. Citations 8-12 are generic canon boilerplate (BIS stablecoin flows, Mauboussin, Marks, Damodaran ERP, Berkshire letters) with no direct relevance to the specific vault or question. Roughly 40-50% on-topic at best. Hallucination: No specific APY figure for the USDC-PYUSD vault is cited without a source, no peg deviation is asserted, and no guaranteed roll-forward yield is claimed. The verdict text itself is minimal (defer, 0.5 confidence) and all agent turns failed with RateLimitError, so no hallucination patterns are present. Dissent grounding: All 7 agent voices failed (RateLimitError), producing zero substantive turns. No expected dissent topics (yield_trap_risk, stablecoin_peg_risk, opportunity_cost) were surfaced in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or any critic turn. dissent_count=0 and blocker_questions=[] confirm this. Confidence calibration: The panel returned 0.5 confidence with all agents failed and no substantive analysis — this is mildly reasonable as a fallback/uncertain posture, but the confidence is arguably slightly high given zero analytical grounding from any agent turn. Mild miscalibration.

rows.judge_notes: Citations 1-7 are directly on-topic (Drift SOL-PERP funding rate data from protocol-native API, grounding the funding rate figures cited). Citations 8-12 are generic canon boilerplate (Fed paper, Mauboussin, Damodaran, Marks, Berkshire) with no specific relevance to the SOL-PERP/Drift/FOMC trade question — dragging citation relevance down significantly. No hallucinated figures detected: funding rates and oracle TWAP prices are sourced from protocol_native citations 1-7. No must_not_hallucinate patterns triggered. However, all 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, meaning zero substantive deliberation occurred. No dissent topics (macro_event_risk, leverage_unwind_risk, funding_rate_risk, liquidation_cascade_risk) were surfaced in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or any critic turn — dissent_count=0 and blocker_questions=[] confirm this. The 'defer' verdict at 0.5 confidence is directionally acceptable but arrived at with no agent reasoning, making confidence calibration poor: the panel cannot justify even 0.5 confidence without any agent analysis, yet the verdict happens to be reasonable. Mild under-confidence relative to what a functioning panel might produce, but given total agent failure the 0.5 is arguably lucky rather than calibrated.

rows.judge_notes: All 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError — no substantive analysis was produced. Citation relevance is very low: only citations 1 (JTO-PERP price, incomplete current price field) and 4-7 (ETH-PERP funding rates, not JTO-PERP) are marginally on-topic for Drift perps; citations 2-3 are for unrelated markets (INJ, JUP); citations 8-12 are completely off-topic canon boilerplate (Oaktree memo, BIS paper, Damodaran, Mauboussin, Berkshire letters). No JTO tokenomics/unlock schedule source present. No hallucinated specific figures were asserted in the verdict text (verdict is bare 'defer' with no supporting claims), so hallucination_score=1.0. Dissent grounding is 0.0: no agent surfaced short_squeeze_risk, funding_rate_risk, or narrative_reversal_risk — all turns were voided. Confidence calibration is mildly off: 0.5 confidence on a completely failed panel with zero substantive analysis is slightly over-confident relative to the information vacuum, but not severely miscalibrated given 0.5 is the midpoint.

rows.judge_notes: Citations 1-7 are marginally relevant (Jupiter/JitoSOL token price data), but citations 8-12 are completely off-topic canon boilerplate (Berkshire letters, Oaktree memos, Damodaran, BIS, Mauboussin) with no bearing on JLP/JitoSOL rotation. No specific ungrounded yield/APY figures were asserted in the verdict text (all agents failed with RateLimitError), so no hallucination pattern triggered. However, all 7 agent turns failed — the panel produced zero substantive analysis, no dissent, no blocker questions, and no surfacing of expected dissent topics (concentration_risk, jlp_drawdown_risk, rotation_timing_risk). Confidence of 0.5 is mildly reasonable given the DEFER verdict but is essentially ungrounded since no agent reasoning exists to support it — mild miscalibration.

rows.judge_notes: Citations: ~5-7 of 12 are on-topic (Jupiter LST price API, bSOL/mSOL token data, Jupiter Lend multiply docs); the rest are canon boilerplate (Berkshire letter, BIS paper, Damodaran divestiture paper, Mauboussin EPS paper, Marks geopolitics memo) with no direct relevance to the LST rotation question — hence ~0.5. Hallucination: no specific ungrounded APY/TVL/points-valuation figures appear in the verdict text; verdict is minimal ('defer', confidence 0.5) with no fabricated numbers, so no must_not_hallucinate patterns triggered — score 1.0. Dissent grounding: all 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, producing zero substantive content; none of the expected dissent topics (points_narrative_risk, peg_deviation_risk, rotation_cost_overhead) were surfaced in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or critic turns — score 0.0. Confidence calibration: 0.5 confidence with zero functioning agent turns and no substantive analysis is mildly reasonable as a fallback but is essentially uncalibrated given the complete panel failure; mild over-confidence relative to the near-zero information content — score 0.5.

rows.judge_notes: Citation relevance: Citations 1-7 are directly on-topic (Jito tip-floor protocol-native API, docs), but citations 8-12 are completely irrelevant canon boilerplate (Berkshire letters, Oaktree, Mauboussin, BIS, Damodaran beta paper) — roughly half the citations are tangential. Hallucination: Citation 5 contains specific SOL lamport figures (P25=0.000001, P50=0.00000208, P75=0.0000053, P95=0.0001, P99=0.00025913) attributed to a protocol_native source dated 2026-05-16, which matches the as_of_date and is grounded in the Jito tip-floor API — this is correct behavior per S34-#77. However, all 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, meaning no agent actually analyzed or cited these figures in reasoning. The verdict text itself does not contain ungrounded specific figures, so no hallucination is scored. Dissent grounding: All 7 agents failed (RateLimitError), producing zero turns with substantive content. None of the expected dissent topics (mempool_depth_volatility, tip_inflation_risk, bot_competition_risk) were surfaced in blocker_questions (empty), surviving dissent (dissent_count=0), or any critic turn. Score = 0.0. Confidence calibration: The panel issued 'defer' at 0.5 confidence with all agents failed and no substantive analysis — deferring with 0.5 is mildly reasonable given total agent failure, but the blocker_questions list is empty despite the panel having no basis to form any view, which is a mild miscalibration. Score = 0.6.

rows.judge_notes: Citation relevance: 7 of 11 citations are on-topic Sanctum protocol docs (INF mechanics, router, reserve, APY endpoint) — solid. However, 4 citations (Damodaran, Berkshire, Oaktree, Mauboussin) are completely unrelated canon boilerplate, dragging relevance down. Hallucination: No specific ungrounded figures (INF/SOL peg, router spread, APY) appear in the verdict text — verdict is just 'defer' with confidence 0.5 and no numeric claims, so no hallucination triggered. Dissent grounding: All 7 agent turns failed with RateLimitError, producing zero substantive content. None of the expected dissent topics (peg_deviation_risk, router_spread_cost, yield_continuity_risk) were surfaced in blocker_questions, surviving dissent, or any critic turn — dissent_count=0 and blocker_questions=[] confirm this. Confidence calibration: DEFER at 0.5 is a reasonable hedge given total agent failure, but the panel produced no analytical content whatsoever — all voices failed. A 0.5 confidence on a completely empty analysis is mildly over-confident; a lower confidence or explicit failure flag would be more appropriate. Mild miscalibration.
